metadata.title: Cellromax Children's Health Syrup | Elderberry Nutrition for Kids
metadata.description: Children's health syrup featuring European elderberry, prebiotics, 7 amino acids, and more. Pharmacy exclusive, from age 1.
metadata.ogDescription: Once a day, nourish your child's health with the power of elderberry
metadata.keywords: children's health syrup,Cellromax,elderberry,kids health,pharmacy exclusive,prebiotics,amino acids

subcopy.ingredients: Elderberry · Prebiotics · Amino Acids · Calcium · Vitamin C · Vitamin B
subcopy.description: 6 essential nutrients in one children's nutrition syrup

body.line2: we formulated the essential nutrients for growing children in one bottle.
body.line3: Cellromax Children's Health Syrup — available only at pharmacies.

headline.line1: The purple berry Europe has cherished for centuries

elderberry.subtitle: Richer in anthocyanins than blueberries

origin.body1: Cellromax Children's Health Syrup uses the Haschberg variety of elderberry cultivated in the Steiermark region of Austria.
origin.body2: A traditional ingredient used for centuries in Europe for seasonal health management.

anthocyanin.title: Anthocyanin — The antioxidant in the purple hue
anthocyanin.intro: Elderberry's deep purple color is evidence of its rich anthocyanin content.
anthocyanin.citation: Source: Wu et al., J Agric Food Chem (2006)
anthocyanin.explanation: Anthocyanins are natural pigments found in plant flowers, fruits, and leaves, belonging to the flavonoid family that creates purple, red, and blue colors. Known as antioxidant nutrients, the deeper the color, the higher the anthocyanin content tends to be. Elderberry is one of the most prominent sources of anthocyanins, with reported levels up to approximately 4.7 times higher than blueberries.

nutritionTable.title: Nutrient-rich elderberry composition
nutritionTable.citation: Source: National Standard Food Composition Table, 'Elderberry, raw'

elderberry.disclaimer: This information describes the raw ingredient, not the product itself

subtitle.line1: 7 amino acids + water-soluble fermented calcium + plant-derived vitamin B
subtitle.line2: Build the foundation for growth with just once a day

radarChart.disclaimer: *This is a conceptual diagram based on the nutritional roles of each ingredient

vitaminBC.title: Vitamins B & C
vitaminBC.desc: From lemon, holy basil, guava & gooseberry

aminoAcid.title: The building blocks of protein: 7 amino acids
aminoAcid.body: A growing child's body creates new cells every day, and amino acids are the foundation. Cellromax contains 7 amino acids including BCAAs (valine, leucine, isoleucine).
aminoAcid.detail: Among them, L-arginine is a semi-essential amino acid known to support healthy development in growing children, and L-glutamine is known as a primary energy source for intestinal mucosal cells.

calcium.title: Water-soluble calcium — that makes the difference
calcium.body: What matters more than how much calcium you take is how well it's absorbed. Cellromax's water-soluble fermented calcium is 100% water-soluble ionized calcium made by naturally fermenting shells from clean ocean waters.
calcium.detail: Extracted through a fermentation process without chemical solvents, it has a form that is favorable for absorption in the body.

vitaminBC.title: Vitamins B & C from plants
vitaminBC.body: Guava, holy basil, and lemon are rich in B vitamins, while Indian gooseberry is abundant in vitamin C.
vitaminBC.detail: Children can get 6 types of vitamin B (B1, B2, B3, B5, B6, B9) and vitamin C all at once.

ingredients.subtitle: Each ingredient selected for its unique properties, designed for children's needs.

duoligo.title: DuOligo — Clinically tested prebiotics
duoligo.body: Contains galacto-oligosaccharides (GOS), known to be found in breast milk, and lactulose — prebiotic ingredients that serve as food for beneficial gut bacteria.
duoligo.detail: In a multi-center clinical trial on infants supplemented with GOS, growth of bifidobacteria and lactobacilli was promoted while harmful bacteria were suppressed.
duoligo.citation: Giovannini et al., J Am Coll Nutr (2014), Multi-center RCT

amla.title: Indian Gooseberry — A vitamin C-rich fruit
amla.body: Amla, a superfood long used in Indian Ayurveda, is known for its exceptionally high vitamin C content.
amla.detail: While regular vitamin C (ascorbic acid) is easily destroyed by heat and light, amla's vitamin C remains stable thanks to its natural protective structure bound with gallic acid and tannins.

monkfruit.title: Monk Fruit — Nature's sweetness
monkfruit.body: Monk Fruit, from the Guilin region of southern China, has been used as a healthful fruit for centuries. It has sweetness 250 times that of sugar, yet with virtually zero calories.
monkfruit.detail: Because this is a product for children, we chose a healthy ingredient even for the sweetener. Monk fruit has received GRAS (Generally Recognized As Safe) status from the U.S. FDA.

subtitle.line1: Globally certified ingredients and pharmaceutical-grade manufacturing.

gmp.desc: Pharmaceutical-grade manufacturing and quality control standards

fssc.desc: International food safety management system certification

iso.desc: International Organization for Standardization quality certification

fssai.desc: Food Safety and Standards Authority of India certification (Indian Gooseberry)

safetyEvidence.title: Safety of Elderberry
safetyEvidence.body: In a clinical trial of 312 participants (Tiralongo et al., 2016), no adverse reactions attributable to elderberry extract were identified.
safetyEvidence.citation: — Tiralongo E, Wee SS, Lea RA. Elderberry Supplementation Reduces Cold Duration and Symptoms in Air-Travellers: A Randomized, Double-Blind Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trial. Nutrients. 2016;8(4):182.

pharmacyOnly.body: Cellromax Children's Health Syrup is a pharmacy-exclusive product. Please consult with a pharmacist to find the right product and form for your child.

subtitle.line1: At home, on the go, in your child's hands.
subtitle.line2: Choose from three formats to suit every situation.

syrup.name: Health Syrup
syrup.tagline: The everyday essential for daily nutrition at home
syrup.desc: An economical large-volume option for consistent nutrition management. 10ml once daily using the included measuring cup.

stick.name: Health Stick
stick.tagline: Portable packs for outings, travel, and daycare
stick.desc: Individually packaged for hygiene, no measuring needed — just give one packet as-is.

jjayo.name: Health Squeeze
jjayo.tagline: Fun squeeze pouches kids can enjoy on their own
jjayo.desc: The squeeze-and-eat experience fosters independence and makes nutrition time fun and resistance-free.
jjayo.extra: One Health Squeeze pouch contains the equivalent of 20ml of the syrup product in a jelly format, recommended for children aged 2 and above.

subtitle.line1: Cellromax Children's Health Syrup is a pharmacy-exclusive product.
subtitle.line2: Search for a nearby pharmacy that carries our products.

search.placeholder: Enter area name or pharmacy name

map.callGuide: Please call us and we'll help you find a nearby pharmacy

pharmacy.notice: Please call the pharmacy to confirm product availability before visiting

items.q: From what age can my child take this?
items.a: Children's Health Syrup (Stick) can be taken from age 1+ / Children's Health Jjayo from age 2+. When starting for the first time, we recommend beginning with a small amount to observe your child's response.

items.q: How much should I give per day?
items.a: We recommend: Health Syrup 10ml once daily / Health Stick 1 packet once daily / Health Jjayo 1 pouch once daily. For the syrup, use the included measuring cup. For sticks and squeeze pouches, one packet is one serving.

items.q: How do I choose between syrup, stick, and squeeze?
items.a: All three formats contain the same key ingredients. If taking daily at home, the syrup (500ml) is most economical. For frequent outings, try the sticks (individual packets). If your child enjoys self-feeding, the squeeze pouches are recommended.
Cellromax Children's Health Syrup is a pharmacy-exclusive product and can only be purchased at your local pharmacy. Use the 'Find a Pharmacy' feature above to search for a nearby location.

items.q: Can it be taken with other supplements?
items.a: It can be consumed with general foods. However, if taking other nutritional supplements with similar ingredients, we recommend consulting with a pharmacist.

Elderberry is a fruit that has been used as a herbal remedy in Europe for a long time, and elderberry extract was safely consumed in human application studies (Tiralongo et al., 2016). However, if your child has a sensitive constitution or allergies, please consult with a pharmacist before consumption.
| This information is a description of the raw ingredient and is not related to the product.

headline.line1: A healthier every day for your child

body.line1: Once a day, a small habit
body.line2: makes a big difference in healthy growth.

disclaimer.title: Notice
items: This product is not a medicine intended for the prevention or treatment of diseases.
Clinical trial results were observed under specific study conditions and individual results may vary.
Please consult with a pharmacist before consumption.
If you have a sensitive constitution or allergies, please check the ingredients before consumption.
